title: The Three Pillars of Object-Oriented Programming
description: A visual representation of the three core principles (Encapsulation, Inheritance, Polymorphism) that form the foundation of OOP and how they interconnect.
relationships: Encapsulation bundles data and methods into a single unit (Class), Inheritance allows new classes (subclasses) to adopt properties of existing ones (superclasses), and Polymorphism enables these subclasses to implement inherited methods in their own way.
